other_observers: None; Dave Elens found the bird (fide Gary Lefko), first posted to Cobirds 6 Jan. by Eric DeFonso
specific_location: Poudre River Trail adjacent to Kodak State Wildlife Area, near 40.1430 N., 105.1424 E.
description: Proportioned like many buteos, perhaps on the smallish side (but difficult to ascertain without direct comparison with another buteo.) Overall brownish tones on back with some scattered whitish gray feather tipping. Tail with wider brown bands interspersed with thinner grayish bands. Primaries fall well short of the end of the tail on the perched bird. Belly white with medium-heavy brownish spotting, growing heavier on breast (but not a solid bib.) Head mostly brownish with pale gray supercillium. Eye pale brown, with yellowish cere and moderately heavy bill (not as stout as Ferruginous but not as delicate-looking as Rough-legged.) Tarsi bare, yellowish. In flight, a pale cinnamon wash pervades the wing lining as seen from below, and a pale crescent crosses at the base of the primaries on the upperwing. The pale crescent grades from whitish on the bases of the outer primaries to more pale cinnamon on the inner primary bases.Behaviors: The bird was flying from perch to perch along the Poudre River, spending a few minutes on each perch (tall cottonwoods.)Call: Heard calling in response to Red-tailed Hawk flying overhead. Richer, distinctive descending calls contrasted with more shrill Red-tail call I often hear.Plumage: juvenile
photographer: Photographer: Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker
